positive.label: Virgin medium blonde — healthy

input.image_description: Young woman with medium blonde hair, natural-looking, no visible regrowth or banding. Hair appears smooth and shiny throughout.

expected_output.description: Medium Blonde, virgin hair, healthy condition

positive.why: No signs of prior colour, consistent level root to tip, healthy cuticle.

positive.label: Brassy grown-out balayage — porous ends

input.stylist_notes: Client says balayage was done about 6 months ago. Has been washing frequently and spending time in the sun.
input.image_description: Woman with dark blonde roots and lighter mids/ends showing gold and orange tones. Ends look slightly dry and matte compared to roots.

expected_output.description: Dark Blonde roots. Grown-out balayage, mids/ends faded to uneven level 8/9 with heavy gold and slight copper (orange). Slightly porous ends.

positive.why: Root level is 6. Mids/ends lifted from previous balayage have faded warm. Porosity noted on ends from prior lightening.

positive.label: Grey blending candidate — mixed condition

input.stylist_notes: Regular client. Last colour was about 8 weeks ago, highlift on roots only. Wants grey covered.
input.image_description: Woman with medium blonde hair showing significant grey at the temples and parting. Mids and ends are a clean level 10 blonde. Ends slightly dry.

expected_output.description: Medium Blonde with 40% scattered grey. Mids/ends previously coloured to level 10 neutral blonde. Normal at root, slightly dry on ends.
expected_output.condition: normal at root, slightly dry ends

positive.why: Root regrowth is natural level 7 with visible grey. Prior colour visible on lengths. Condition varies root vs ends.

negative.label: Warm blonde misread as light brown

input.image_description: Woman with warm-toned blonde hair in indoor tungsten lighting. Hair has golden undertones throughout.

bad_output.description: Light brown hair, natural, healthy condition

negative.correction: Level 5 is too dark. Warm blonde with gold undertones in tungsten lighting is still level 7, not light brown. The gold tone is an undertone, not an indicator of a lower level. Account for warm indoor lighting shifting perception darker.

negative.label: Grey percentage underestimated

input.stylist_notes: Client mentions greys coming through at the roots.
input.image_description: Woman with blonde hair, grey visible at temples and through the parting line. Some scattered grey strands visible when hair is parted.

bad_output.description: Medium Blonde with minimal grey at temples

negative.correction: Grey at temples AND scattered through the parting typically indicates 30-40%, not 10%. Grey blends with blonde and is easy to undercount in photos. When grey is visible at both the hairline and parting, always estimate higher. A 10% call would lead to a formulation that skips grey coverage entirely.

negative.label: Porosity missed on previously coloured ends

input.image_description: Woman with dark roots and lighter ends. Ends look matte and slightly frizzy compared to smooth roots.

bad_output.description: Dark Blonde with lighter balayage ends, good overall condition

negative.correction: Matte, frizzy ends on previously lightened hair indicate porosity. Reporting 'healthy' when ends are visibly porous leads to over-processing during toning — porous hair grabs colour faster and darker. Condition should note 'slightly porous ends' or 'porous ends' so the formulation accounts for it (shorter processing time, careful monitoring).
